description: Alerting rules for the Azure Data & Analytics Pipeline chained event scenario

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure Data Pipeline — High Failure Rate
rules.investigationGuide:
# High Failure Rate

The orchestrator is logging more failures than normal.

## Triage
1. Are failures on one pipeline? Run: `FROM logs-azure.datafactory-* | WHERE event.outcome == "failure" | STATS failures = COUNT(*) BY azure.datafactory.pipeline_name | SORT failures DESC | LIMIT 10`
2. Check linked dashboard for the spike shape
3. Check if Databricks/Spark or Blob Storage rules also fired

## Escalate when
- Multiple pipelines failing
- Failures still rising after 15 min
- SLA-bound data affected

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure Data Pipeline — Null/Empty Data Detected
rules.investigationGuide:
# Null/Empty Data Detected

A query returned zero rows — the upstream load may have silently failed.

## Triage
1. Identify the empty query: `FROM logs-azure.synapse-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND azure.synapse.rows_returned == 0 | KEEP @timestamp, azure.synapse.query_id, azure.synapse.pool_name | SORT @timestamp DESC | LIMIT 25`
2. Check the source partition exists
3. If orchestrator shows "success" but query is empty — silent data loss

## Escalate when
- Query feeds an SLA-bound dataset
- Empty for two consecutive runs

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure Data Pipeline — Databricks/Spark Processing Error
rules.investigationGuide:
# Databricks/Spark Processing Error

The compute cluster is logging exceptions.

## Triage
1. Classify errors: `FROM logs-azure.databricks-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND error.type IS NOT NULL | STATS errors = COUNT(*) BY error.type, host.id | SORT errors DESC | LIMIT 20`
2. Same host failing = infra; many hosts same error = code/data
3. Check if High Failure Rate also fired

## Escalate when
- Multiple clusters affected
- Same error after cluster restart

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure Data Pipeline — Blob Source File Format Error
rules.investigationGuide:
# Blob Storage Source File Format Error

An unexpected file format landed in the source container.

## Triage
1. List bad files: `FROM logs-azure.blobstorage-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h | WHERE NOT ENDS_WITH(azure.blobstorage.blob_name, ".avro") OR azure.blobstorage.blob_name LIKE "*%*" | KEEP @timestamp, azure.blobstorage.blob_name, azure.blobstorage.container_name | SORT @timestamp DESC | LIMIT 50`
2. Check if one producer or many
3. Move bad file to quarantine path

## Escalate when
- Multiple producers writing wrong format
- Blob versioning overwritten

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure Data Pipeline — Slow Pipeline Run (>60s)
rules.investigationGuide:
# Slow Pipeline Run (>60s)

A pipeline exceeded its informal SLA.

## Triage
1. Compare to baseline: `FROM logs-azure.datafactory-* | WHERE azure.data_factory.activity_name == "pipeline_complete" AND @timestamp > NOW() - 7d | STATS p50 = PERCENTILE(azure.datafactory.duration_ms, 50), p95 = PERCENTILE(azure.datafactory.duration_ms, 95) BY azure.datafactory.pipeline_name | WHERE p95 > 30000 | SORT p95 DESC`
2. If within 2x of p95, likely noise
3. Check Databricks errors and input volume

## Escalate when
- SLA-bound pipeline slow for two consecutive runs
- Correlated with executor errors
